121→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
122→
123→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
124→
125→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
126→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
127→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
128→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
129→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
130→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
131→
132→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
133→
134→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
135→
136→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
137→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
138→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
139→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
140→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
141→
142→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
143→
144→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
145→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
146→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
147→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
148→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
149→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
150→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
151→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
152→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
153→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
154→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
155→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
156→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
157→
158→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
159→
160→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
161→
162→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
163→
164→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
165→
166→#### Experimental rules
167→
168→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
169→
170→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
171→
172→#### EditorConfig
173→
174→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
175→
176→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
177→
178→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
179→
180→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
181→
182→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
183→
184→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
185→
186→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
187→
188→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
189→
190→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
191→
192→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
193→
194→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
195→
196→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
197→
198→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
199→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
200→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |

231→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
232→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
233→        return when {
234→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
235→            else -> "\n"
236→        }
237→    }
238→
239→    /**
240→     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
241→     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
242→     *
243→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
244→     * ```properties
245→     * final-newline=true
246→     * indent-size=4
247→     * ```
248→     */
249→    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
250→        val codeStyle =
251→            editorConfigOverride
252→                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
253→                ?.parsed
254→                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
255→                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
256→        val rules =
257→            ruleProviders
258→                .map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
259→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
260→                .toSet()
261→        return EditorConfigGenerator(fileSystem, editorConfigLoaderEc4j)
262→            .generateEditorconfig(
263→                rules,
264→                codeStyle,
265→                filePath,
266→            )
267→    }
268→
269→    /**
270→     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
271→     */
272→    public fun trimMemory() {
273→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.clear()
274→    }
275→
276→    /**
277→     * Get the list of files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting the given file or directory.
278→     * The API consumer can use this list to observe changes in '.editorconfig' files. Whenever such a change is
279→     * observed, the API consumer should call [reloadEditorConfigFile].
280→     * To avoid unnecessary access to the file system, it is best to call this method only once for the root of the
281→     * project which is to be [lint] or [format].
282→     */
283→    public fun editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path> = EditorConfigFinder(editorConfigLoaderEc4j).findEditorConfigs(path)
284→
285→    /**
286→     * Reloads an '.editorconfig' file given that it is currently loaded into the KtLint cache. This method is intended
287→     * to be called by the API consumer when it is aware of changes in the '.editorconfig' file that should be taken
288→     * into account with next calls to [lint] and/or [format]. See [editorConfigFilePaths] to get the list of
289→     * '.editorconfig' files which need to be observed.
290→     */
291→    public fun reloadEditorConfigFile(path: Path) {
292→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.reloadIfExists(
293→            Resource.Resources.ofPath(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8),
294→        )
295→    }

297→    public fun transformToAst(code: Code): FileASTNode = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code).rootNode

299→    private fun LintError.logMessage(code: Code) =
300→        "${code.fileNameOrStdin()}:$line:$col: $detail ($ruleId)" +
301→            if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
302→                ""
303→            } else {
304→                " [cannot be autocorrected]"
305→            }

308→        internal const val UTF8_BOM = "\uFEFF"
309→
310→        public const val STDIN_FILE: String = "<stdin>"
311→
312→        private const val MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE = 3
313→    }
314→}

**Planning documentation updates for default code style change**

I’m sorting out which docs and code comments need updating to reflect that the default code style is now `ktlint_official` instead of `intellij_idea`. I’m focusing mainly on the specified files but also considering broader references, especially around `CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue` and any TODOs or tests that mention future changes. The goal is a minimal yet thorough update to ensure consistency without touching existing tests. I’ll start with `CodeStyleEditorConfigProperty.kt` and then check tests like `EditorConfigTest` for needed adjustments.

241→        // The Property and PropertyValue builders of the ec4j library do not allow to build a property having field "Property.type" set to
242→        // null. However, when loading an ".editorconfig" file containing a property with a name for which no property type exists with that
243→        // same name, this does result in such a property. It takes a detour to create the property:
244→        //  - Create an ".editorconfig" file on the file system mock
245→        //  - Load the ".editorconfig" via the EditorConfigDefaults loader
246→        //  - Find the property which is set in the ".editorconfig" file and validate that its type is null
247→        //  - Transform the property to a KtLint EditorConfig
248→        //  - Extract the property of the KtLint EditorConfig
249→        // Note that two properties are created which are identical except that only one of the properties has a null type and the other
250→        // property has a correctly defined type.
251→        //language=
252→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = "ktlint_test_rule-1"
253→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1)
254→        //language=
255→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = "ktlint_test_rule-2"
256→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2)

259→            KtlintTestFileSystem().apply {
260→                writeRootEditorConfigFile(
261→                    //language=EditorConfig
262→                    """
263→                    [*.{kt,kts}]
264→                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = disabled
265→                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = disabled
266→                    """.trimIndent(),
267→                )
268→            }
269→
270→        val ktlintTestRuleProperties =
271→            EditorConfigDefaults
272→                .load(
273→                    path = ktlintTestFileSystem.resolve(""),
274→                    propertyTypes =
275→                        setOf(
276→                            // Note that ktlintTestRuleEditorConfigPropertyType1 has been left out on purpose
277→                            ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2.type,
278→                        ),
279→                ).value
280→                .sections
281→                .flatMap { it.properties.values }
282→                .associateBy { it.name }
283→
284→        // Validate that both properties are loaded
285→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1] != null) {
286→            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
287→        }
288→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2] != null) {
289→            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
290→        }
291→
292→        // Validate that the type of one of the properties is null
293→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1]?.type == null) {
294→            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' should have an undefined type"
295→        }
296→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2]?.type != null) {
297→            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' should have a defined type"
298→        }
299→
300→        val editorConfig = EditorConfig(ktlintTestRuleProperties)
301→
302→        // Although the type of one of the properties is null, both can be loaded as the type of EditorConfigProperty is used to parse the
303→        // raw value of the property
304→        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
305→        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
306→    }
307→
308→    private fun editorConfigProperty(name: String) =
309→        EditorConfigProperty(
310→            name = name,
311→            type =
312→                PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
313→                    name,
314→                    "",
315→                    SafeEnumValueParser(RuleExecution::class.java),
316→                    RuleExecution.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
317→                ),
318→            defaultValue = RuleExecution.enabled,
319→        )
320→
321→    private companion object {
322→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_NAME = "some-property-name"
323→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE = "some-property-value"
324→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO = "some-property-value-android"
325→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT = "some-property-value-default"
326→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA = "some-property-value-intellij-idea"
327→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL = "some-property-value-official"
328→        val SOME_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY =
329→            EditorConfigProperty(
330→                name = SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
331→                type =
332→                    PropertyType(
333→                        SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
334→                        "",
335→                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.IDENTITY_VALUE_PARSER,
336→                        setOf(SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO, SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA),
337→                    ),
338→                defaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT,
339→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO,
340→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL,
341→                intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA,
342→            )
343→    }
344→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine.Companion.UTF8_BOM
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.InternalRuleProvidersFilter
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RuleExecutionRuleFilter
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RunAfterRuleFilter
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.applyRuleFilters
 17→import mu.KotlinLogging
 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.FileASTNode
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiErrorElement
 22→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.idea.KotlinLanguage
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFile
 24→import kotlin.io.path.pathString
 25→
 26→private val KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER = KotlinPsiFileFactoryProvider()
 27→
 28→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 29→
 30→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 31→    val code: Code,
 32→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 33→    val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
 34→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 35→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 36→) {
 37→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 38→
 39→    init {
 40→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 44→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode, editorConfig)
 45→    }
 46→
 47→    fun executeRule(
 48→        rule: Rule,
 49→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 50→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 51→    ) {
 52→        try {
 53→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 54→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 55→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 56→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 57→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 58→                // Note that also the CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY is provided because that property is needed to determine the default value of an
 59→                // EditorConfigProperty that is not explicitly defined.
 60→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties.plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY)),
 61→            )
 62→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, autoCorrect, emit)
 63→            rule.afterLastNode()
 64→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 65→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 66→                e.line,
 67→                e.col,
 68→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 69→                """
 70→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 71→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 72→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 73→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 74→                """.trimIndent(),
 75→                e.cause,
 76→            )
 77→        }
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        rule: Rule,
 83→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 84→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 85→    ) {
 86→        /**
 87→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 88→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 89→         */
 90→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 92→            try {
 93→                executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(node, rule, suppressHandler)
 94→            } catch (e: Exception) {
 95→                if (autoCorrect) {
 96→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
 97→                    // original AST
 98→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
 99→                        rule,
100→                        0,
101→                        0,
102→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
103→                        e.cause ?: e,
104→                    )
105→                } else {
106→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
107→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
108→                        rule,
109→                        line,
110→                        col,
111→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
112→                        e.cause ?: e,
113→                    )
114→                }
115→            }
116→        }
117→    }
118→
119→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
120→        node: ASTNode,
121→        rule: Rule,
122→        suppressHandler: SuppressHandler,
123→    ) {
124→        suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
125→            rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
126→        }
127→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
128→            node
129→                .getChildren(null)
130→                .forEach { childNode ->
131→                    suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
132→                        this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
133→                            childNode,
134→                            rule,
135→                            autoCorrect,
136→                            emit,
137→                        )
138→                    }
139→                }
140→        }
141→        suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
142→            rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
143→        }
144→    }
145→
146→    companion object {
147→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
148→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
149→            code: Code,
150→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
151→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine)
152→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
153→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
154→
155→            val psiFileName =
156→                code
157→                    .filePath
158→                    ?.pathString
159→                    ?: if (code.script) {
160→                        "File.kts"
161→                    } else {
162→                        "File.kt"
163→                    }
164→            val psiFile =
165→                psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
166→                    psiFileName,
167→                    KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
168→                    normalizedText,
169→                ) as KtFile
170→            psiFile
171→                .findErrorElement()
172→                ?.let { errorElement ->
173→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
174→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
175→                }
176→
177→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
178→
179→            val editorConfig =
180→                ktLintRuleEngine
181→                    .editorConfigLoader
182→                    .load(code.filePath)
183→                    .also {
184→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
185→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
186→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
187→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
188→                    }
189→
190→            val ruleProviders =
191→                ktLintRuleEngine
192→                    .applyRuleFilters(
193→                        InternalRuleProvidersFilter(ktLintRuleEngine),
194→                        RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
195→                        RunAfterRuleFilter(),
196→                    )
197→
198→            return RuleExecutionContext(
199→                code,
200→                rootNode,
201→                ruleProviders,
202→                editorConfig,
203→                positionInTextLocator,
204→            )
205→        }
206→
207→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
208→            return text
209→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
210→                .replace("\r", "\n")
211→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
212→        }
213→
214→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
215→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
216→                return this
217→            }
218→            this.children.forEach { child ->
219→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
220→                if (errorElement != null) {
221→                    return errorElement
222→                }
223→            }
224→            return null
225→        }
226→    }
227→}
228→
229→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
230→    propertyName: String,
231→    ktlintVersion: String,
232→) {
233→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
234→        LOGGER.warn {
235→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
236→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
237→        }
238→    }
239→}
240→
241→private class RuleExecutionException(
242→    val rule: Rule,
243→    val line: Int,
244→    val col: Int,
245→    override val cause: Throwable,
246→) : Throwable(cause)
247→

  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger

  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ec4j.toPropertyWithValue
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.FormatterTags.Companion.FORMATTER_TAGS_ENABLED_PROPERTY
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.FormatterTags.Companion.FORMATTER_TAG_OFF_ENABLED_PROPERTY
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.FormatterTags.Companion.FORMATTER_TAG_ON_ENABLED_PROPERTY
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.ThreadSafeEditorConfigCache.Companion.THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE
 20→import mu.KotlinLogging
 21→import org.ec4j.core.EditorConfigLoader
 22→import org.ec4j.core.PropertyTypeRegistry
 23→import org.ec4j.core.Resource
 24→import org.ec4j.core.ResourcePropertiesService
 25→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
 26→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 27→import org.ec4j.core.model.Version
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.applyIf
 29→import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets
 30→import java.nio.file.FileSystem
 31→import java.nio.file.FileSystems
 32→import java.nio.file.Path
 33→
 34→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 35→
 36→/**
 37→ * Loader for `.editorconfig` properties for files on [fileSystem].
 38→ */
 39→internal class EditorConfigLoader(
 40→    private val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 41→    private val editorConfigLoaderEc4j: EditorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 42→    private val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 43→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 44→) {
 45→    /**
 46→     * Loads properties used by [Rule]s from the `.editorconfig` file on given [filePath]. When [filePath] is null, the
 47→     * properties for the ".kt" pattern in the current directory are loaded. The '.editorconfig' files on the [filePath]
 48→     * are read starting from the [filePath] upwards until an '.editorconfig' file is found in which the property "root"
 49→     * is found with value "true" or until the root of the filesystem is reached.
 50→     *
 51→     * Properties specified in [editorConfigDefaults] will be used in case the property was not found in any
 52→     * '.editorconfig' on [filePath]. If the property is not specified in [editorConfigDefaults] then the default value
 53→     * as specified in the property definition [EditorConfigProperty] is used.
 54→     *
 55→     * Properties specified in [editorConfigOverride] take precedence above any other '.editorconfig' file on [filePath]
 56→     * or default value.
 57→     */
 58→    internal fun load(filePath: Path?): EditorConfig {
 59→        val editorConfigPath = filePath ?: defaultFilePath()
 60→        val editorConfigProperties: MutableMap<String, Property> =
 61→            createResourcePropertiesService(editorConfigLoaderEc4j.editorConfigLoader, editorConfigDefaults)
 62→                .queryProperties(editorConfigPath.resource())
 63→                .properties
 64→        return editorConfigProperties
 65→            .also { properties ->
 66→                if (properties[TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME]?.sourceValue == properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.name]?.sourceValue &&
 67→                    editorConfigOverride.properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY] != null
 68→                ) {
 69→                    // The tab_width property can not be overridden via the editorConfigOverride. So if it has been
 70→                    // set to the same value as the indent_size property then keep its value in sync with that
 71→                    // property.
 72→                    properties[TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME] =
 73→                        Property
 74→                            .builder()
 75→                            .name(TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME)
 76→                            .type(PropertyType.tab_width)
 77→                            .value(editorConfigOverride.properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY]?.source)
 78→                            .build()
 79→                }
 80→                editorConfigOverride
 81→                    .properties
 82→                    .forEach {
 83→                        properties[it.key.name] = it.key.toPropertyWithValue(it.value)
 84→                    }
 85→            }.also { properties ->
 86→                LOGGER.debug { properties.prettyPrint(filePath) }
 87→            }.let { properties ->
 88→                // Only add properties which are not related to rules but which are needed by the KtLint Rule Engine
 89→                EditorConfig(properties)
 90→                    .addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(
 91→                        /*
 92→                         * Used in [EditorConfig] to determine the default value for a property when it is not specified in the
 93→                         * [EditorConfig].
 94→                         */
 95→                        CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 96→                        /*
 97→                         * Used by [KtLintRuleEngine] to use correct line separator when writing the formatted output.
 98→                         */
 99→                        END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY,
100→                        /*
101→                         * Used by [VisitorProvider] to determine whether experimental rules have to be executed.
102→                         */
103→                        EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY,
104→                        /*
105→                         * Used by [FormatterTags] to determine whether formatter tags should be respected.
106→                         */
107→                        FORMATTER_TAGS_ENABLED_PROPERTY,
108→                        /*
109→                         * Used by [FormatterTags] to get the tag to disable the formatter.
110→                         */
111→                        FORMATTER_TAG_OFF_ENABLED_PROPERTY,
112→                        /*
113→                         * Used by [FormatterTags] to get the tag to enable the formatter.
114→                         */
115→                        FORMATTER_TAG_ON_ENABLED_PROPERTY,
116→                    )
117→            }
118→    }
119→
120→    private fun MutableMap<String, Property>.prettyPrint(normalizedFilePath: Path?) =
121→        map { entry -> "${entry.key}: ${entry.value.sourceValue}" }
122→            .joinToString(
123→                prefix =
124→                    "Effective editorconfig properties${
125→                        if (normalizedFilePath == null) {
126→                            ""
127→                        } else {
128→                            " for file '$normalizedFilePath'"
129→                        }
130→                    }:\n\t",
131→                separator = "\n\t",
132→            )
133→
134→    private fun Path?.resource() = Resource.Resources.ofPath(this, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
135→
136→    private fun defaultFilePath() =
137→        fileSystem
138→            .getPath(".")
139→            .toAbsolutePath()
140→            .resolve(SUPPORTED_FILES.first())
141→
142→    private fun createResourcePropertiesService(
143→        editorConfigLoader: EditorConfigLoader,
144→        editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults,
145→    ) = ResourcePropertiesService
146→        .builder()
147→        .keepUnset(true)
148→        .cache(THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE)
149→        .loader(editorConfigLoader)
150→        .applyIf(editorConfigDefaults != EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS) {
151→            defaultEditorConfigs(editorConfigDefaults.value)
152→        }.build()
153→
154→    companion object {
155→        /**
156→         * List of file extensions, editorconfig lookup will be performed.
157→         */
158→        internal val SUPPORTED_FILES =
159→            arrayOf(
160→                ".kt",
161→                ".kts",
162→            )
163→
164→        private const val TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME = "tab_width"
165→    }
166→}
167→
168→/**
169→ * Wrapper around the native EditorConfigLoader of the ec4j library. Whenever Ktlints loads ".editorconfig" files via the ec4j library, it
170→ * needs to be aware of all native (ec4j) but also the custom property types defined in KtLint and the custom rules sets provided to KtLint.
171→ *
172→ * The ec4j library uses the convention that a property type has a name that is identical to the name of that property. If no
173→ * property type is registered for a property the property will be returned with a null type. When retrieving the property value it will be
174→ * returned as type "String" which results in class cast exceptions in KtLint's EditorConfig whenever the expected type of the variable is
175→ * not of type String.
176→ */
177→internal class EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(
178→    private val propertyTypes: Set<PropertyType<*>>,
179→) {
180→    val editorConfigLoader: EditorConfigLoader
181→        get() = EditorConfigLoader.of(Version.CURRENT, propertyTypeRegistry(propertyTypes))
182→
183→    private fun propertyTypeRegistry(propertyTypes: Set<PropertyType<*>>) =
184→        PropertyTypeRegistry
185→            .builder()
186→            .defaults()
187→            .apply {
188→                propertyTypes.forEach { type(it) }
189→            }.build()
190→}
191→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ALL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.RULE_EXECUTION_PROPERTY_TYPE
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.RuleExecution
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rules.KTLINT_SUPPRESSION_RULE_ID
 18→import mu.KotlinLogging
 19→
 20→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 21→
 22→/**
 23→ * Filters the [RuleProvider]s defined in the [KtLintRuleEngine] for [Rule]s which are enabled for the given [EditorConfig].
 24→ */
 25→internal class RuleExecutionRuleFilter(
 26→    private val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 27→) : RuleFilter {
 28→    override fun filter(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Set<RuleProvider> =
 29→        if (disableKtlintEntirely()) {
 30→            emptySet()
 31→        } else {
 32→            val ruleExecutionFilter =
 33→                RuleExecutionFilter(
 34→                    editorConfig.ruleExecutionProperties(ruleProviders),
 35→                    editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 36→                )
 37→            ruleProviders
 38→                .filter { ruleExecutionFilter.isEnabled(it) }
 39→                .toSet()
 40→        }
 41→
 42→    private fun disableKtlintEntirely() =
 43→        editorConfig.getEditorConfigValueOrNull(RULE_EXECUTION_PROPERTY_TYPE, ALL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name) == RuleExecution.disabled
 44→
 45→    private fun EditorConfig.ruleExecutionProperties(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Map<String, RuleExecution> {
 46→        val ruleExecutionPropertyNames =
 47→            ruleExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders)
 48→                .plus(ruleSetExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders))
 49→                .plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name)
 50→        return map { it.name to it.sourceValue }
 51→            .toMap()
 52→            .filterKeys { it in ruleExecutionPropertyNames }
 53→            .mapValues { RULE_EXECUTION_PROPERTY_TYPE.parse(it.value).parsed }
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    private fun ruleExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>) =
 57→        ruleProviders
 58→            .map { it.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName() }
 59→            .distinct()
 60→
 61→    private fun ruleSetExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>) =
 62→        ruleProviders
 63→            .map { it.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName() }
 64→            .distinct()
 65→}
 66→
 67→/**
 68→ * Creates a filter of [RuleExecution] properties. This filter is basically an extract of the [EditorConfig] which allows to retrieve rule
 69→ * execution properties for which no proper [EditorConfigProperty] is defined as rules have no need to be able to extract such a property
 70→ * from [EditorConfig].
 71→ */
 72→private class RuleExecutionFilter(
 73→    val ruleExecutionProperties: Map<String, RuleExecution>,
 74→    val codeStyleValue: CodeStyleValue,
 75→) {
 76→    fun isEnabled(ruleProvider: RuleProvider) = isRuleEnabled(ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance())
 77→
 78→    private fun isRuleEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 79→        /*
 80→         * If set for the rule, the rule execution property takes precedence above other checks. This allows for execution of a specific
 81→         * experimental or ktlint_official code style rule without enabling them all. Also, this allows to disable a specific rule in case
 82→         * the experimental and/or ktlint_official code style rules are enabled.
 83→         */
 84→        ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName())
 85→            ?.let {
 86→                if (rule.ruleId == KTLINT_SUPPRESSION_RULE_ID && it == RuleExecution.disabled) {
 87→                    LOGGER.warn { "Rule '${rule.ruleId.value}' can not be disabled via the '.editorconfig'" }
 88→                    true
 89→                } else {
 90→                    it == RuleExecution.enabled
 91→                }
 92→            }
 93→            ?: isRuleConditionallyEnabled(rule)
 94→
 95→    private fun isRuleConditionallyEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 96→        when {
 97→            rule is Rule.Experimental && rule is Rule.OfficialCodeStyle ->
 98→                isExperimentalEnabled(rule) && isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule)
 99→            rule is Rule.Experimental ->
100→                isExperimentalEnabled(rule)
101→            rule is Rule.OfficialCodeStyle ->
102→                isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule)
103→            else ->
104→                isRuleSetEnabled(rule)
105→        }
106→
107→    private fun isExperimentalEnabled(rule: Rule) =
108→        ruleExecution(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name) == RuleExecution.enabled &&
109→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled &&
110→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled
111→
112→    private fun isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule: Rule) =
113→        codeStyleValue == CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official &&
114→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled &&
115→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled
116→
117→    private fun isRuleSetEnabled(rule: Rule) =
118→        ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName())
119→            .let { ruleSetExecution ->
120→                if (ruleSetExecution?.name == EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name) {
121→                    // Rules in the experimental rule set are only run when enabled explicitly.
122→                    ruleSetExecution == RuleExecution.enabled
123→                } else {
124→                    // Rules in other rule sets are enabled by default.
125→                    ruleSetExecution != RuleExecution.disabled
126→                }
127→            }
128→
129→    private fun ruleExecution(ruleExecutionPropertyName: String) = ruleExecutionProperties[ruleExecutionPropertyName]
130→}
131→

conversation_history.content:
1→In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2→
 3→```ini
 4→[*.{kt,kts}]
 5→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6→```
 7→
 8→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9→
10→!!! note
11→    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12→
13→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14→
15→* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
16→
17→* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18→

conversation_history.content:
1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
 8→ */
 9→@Suppress("EnumEntryName")
10→public enum class CodeStyleValue {
11→    /**
12→     * Code formatting based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This
13→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in Android Studio.
14→     */
15→    android_studio,
16→
17→    /**
18→     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
19→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
20→     */
21→    intellij_idea,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
25→     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
26→     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
27→     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
28→     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
29→     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
30→     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
31→     * are still not fixed.
32→     * Starting with ktlint 1.0, this is the default code style provided by KtLint.
33→     */
34→    ktlint_official,
35→}
36→
37→public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
38→    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
39→        "ktlint_code_style",
40→        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. " +
41→            "Starting with ktlint 1.0 the value defaults to 'ktlint_official'. Specify another value if " +
42→            "you want to opt into a different code style.",
43→SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
44→        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
45→    )
46→
47→public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<CodeStyleValue> =
48→    EditorConfigProperty(
49→        type = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE,
50→        /*
51→         * Starting with ktlint 1.0 the [CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official] code style is the default. Configure a
52→         * different value explicitly to retain the legacy behavior.
53→         */
54→        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
55→androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.android_studio,
56→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
57→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
58→    )
59→

* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
* As a part of public API stabilization, configure `binary-compatibility-validator` plugin for compile-time verification of binary compatibility with previous `ktlint` versions ([#2131](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2131))
","new_string":"### Changed

* Change the default `ktlint_code_style` to `ktlint_official`. Configure `intellij_idea` or `android_studio` explicitly to retain the legacy formatting behavior.
* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
* As a part of public API stabilization, configure `binary-compatibility-validator` plugin for compile-time verification of binary compatibility with previous `ktlint` versions ([#2131](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2131))
"}

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
  2→
  3→!!! danger
  4→
  5→    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
  6→
  7→## Code style
  8→
  9→By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
 10→
 11→```ini
 12→[*.{kt,kts}]
 13→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 14→```
 15→
 16→!!! note
 17→    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
 18→
 19→## Disabled rules
 20→
 21→!!! note
 22→    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
 23→
 24→Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
 25→
 26→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 27→```editorconfig
 28→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 29→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
 30→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 31→```
 32→
 33→Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
 34→```editorconfig
 35→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 36→```
 37→
 38→An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 39→```editorconfig
 40→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
 41→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 42→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 43→```
 44→
 45→!!! note
 46→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 47→
 48→
 49→## Final newline
 50→
 51→By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
 52→
 53→```ini
 54→[*.{kt,kts}]
 55→insert_final_newline = true
 56→```
 57→
 58→This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
 59→
 60→## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters
 61→
 62→Setting `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` forces a multiline function signature in case the function contains the specified minimum number of parameters even in case the function signature would fit on a single line. Use value `unset` (default) to disable this setting.
 63→
 64→!!! note
 65→    By default, the `ktlint_official` code style wraps parameters of functions having at least 2 parameters. For other code styles, this setting is disabled by default. 
 66→
 67→```ini
 68→[*.{kt,kts}]
 69→ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than=unset
 70→```
 71→
 72→This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
 73→
 74→## Wrapping the expression body of a function
 75→
 76→Setting `ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping` determines if and when the expression body of a function is wrapped to a new line. This setting can be set to value `default`, `multiline` or `always`. 
 77→
 78→When set to `default`, the first line of a body expression is appended to the function signature as long as the max line length is not exceeded.
 79→
 80→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=default (or when not set)"
 81→// Given that the function signature has to be written as a single line function signature
 82→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 83→    .uppercase()
 84→
 85→// Given that the function signature has to be written as a multiline function signature
 86→fun someFunction(
 87→    a: Any,
 88→    b: Any
 89→): String = "some-result"
 90→    .uppercase()
 91→```
 92→
 93→When set to `multiline`, the body expression starts on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
 94→
 95→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=multiline"
 96→// Given a single line body expression and
 97→// a the function signature that has to be written as a single line function signature and
 98→// it does not exceed the max line length
 99→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result".uppercase()
100→
101→// Given a single line body expression and
102→// a the function signature that has to be written as a multiline function signature and
103→// it does not exceed the max line length
104→fun someFunction(
105→    a: Any,
106→    b: Any
107→): String = "some-result".uppercase()
108→
109→// Given a single line body expression then always wrap it to a separate line
110→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
111→    "some-result"
112→         .uppercase()
113→fun someFunction(
114→    a: Any,
115→    b: Any
116→): String =
117→    "some-result"
118→       .uppercase()
119→```
120→
121→When set to `always` the body expression is always wrapped to a separate line.
122→
123→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=always"
124→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
125→    "some-result".uppercase()
126→fun functionWithAVeryLongName(
127→    a: Any,
128→    b: Any
129→): String =
130→    "some-result"
131→        .uppercase()
132→```
133→
134→This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
135→
136→## Ignore identifiers enclosed in backticks
137→
138→By default, the identifiers enclosed in backticks are not ignored.
139→
140→According to [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) it is acceptable to write method names in natural language. When using natural language, the description tends to be longer. This property allows lines containing an identifier between backticks to be longer than the maximum line length. (Since 0.41.0)
141→
142→```kotlin
143→@Test
144→fun `Given a test with a very loooooooooooooooooooooong test description`() {
145→    
146→}
147→```
148→
149→```ini
150→[*.{kt,kts}]
151→ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier = false
152→```
153→
154→This setting only takes effect when rule `max-line-length` is enabled.
155→
156→## Import layouts
157→
158→By default, the same imports are allowed as in IntelliJ IDEA. The import path can be a full path, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard path, e.g. "kotlin.**".
159→
160→The layout can be composed by the following symbols:
161→
162→*  `*` - wildcard. There must be at least one entry of a single wildcard to match all other imports. Matches anything after a specified symbol/import as well.
163→* `|` - blank line. Supports only single blank lines between imports. No blank line is allowed in the beginning or end of the layout.
164→* `^` - alias import, e.g. "^android.*" will match all android alias imports, "^" will match all other alias imports.
165→
166→Examples:
167→```kotlin
168→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=* # alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a), no blank lines
169→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^ # default IntelliJ IDEA style, same as alphabetical, but with "java", "javax", "kotlin" and alias imports in the end of the imports list
170→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=android.**,|,^org.junit.**,kotlin.io.Closeable.*,|,*,^ # custom imports layout
171→```
172→
173→Wildcard imports can be allowed for specific import paths (Comma-separated list, use "**" as wildcard for package and all subpackages). This setting overrides the no-wildcard-imports rule. This setting is best be used for allowing wildcard imports from libraries like Ktor where extension functions are used in a way that creates a lot of imports.
174→
175→```ini
176→[*.{kt,kts}]
177→ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**
178→```
179→
180→This setting only takes effect when rule `no-wildcard-imports` is enabled.
181→
182→## Indent size & style
183→
184→By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level. Code style `android_studio` uses a tab per indent level.
185→
186→```ini
187→[*.{kt,kts}]
188→indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
189→indent_style = space # or "tab"
190→```
191→
192→Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
193→
194→## Max line length
195→
196→By default, the maximum line length is not set. The `android` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide).
197→
198→```ini
199→[*.{kt,kts}]
200→max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length

content.thinking:
**Preparing comprehensive final summary**

I'm consolidating all the changes made: updating the default `CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY` to `ktlint_official`, adjusting documentation in both release and snapshot versions, adding a changelog entry, and modifying tests. I'll also note that `ClassSignatureRule` remains unchanged but naturally adopts the new default due to shared properties. Finally, I'll mention that tests were not run as per instructions and include instructions for users wanting the previous defaults.

content.text:
Summary:
- Switched the `ktlint_code_style` editorconfig property default to `ktlint_official`, refreshed its documentation strings, and clarified that opting into `intellij_idea` or `android_studio` now requires an explicit configuration.  
- Documented the new default by updating the Unreleased changelog entry plus the “code styles” and “configuration” docs in both the `release-latest` and `snapshot` trees so users know how to retain the former behavior.  
- Adjusted the `EditorConfigTest` expectation to reflect the new default code style so the test suite continues to validate the correct fallback value.

Testing:
- Not run (not requested).

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
 8→ */
 9→@Suppress("EnumEntryName")
10→public enum class CodeStyleValue {
11→    /**
12→     * Code formatting based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This
13→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in Android Studio.
14→     */
15→    android_studio,
16→
17→    /**
18→     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
19→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
20→     */
21→    intellij_idea,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
25→     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
26→     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
27→     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
28→     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
29→     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
30→     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
31→     * are still not fixed.
32→     * In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
33→     */
34→    ktlint_official,
35→}
36→
37→public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
38→    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
39→        "ktlint_code_style",
40→        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. Currently, the " +
41→            "value is defaulted to 'intellij_idea' when not set. However, in the future the default code style " +
42→            "will be changed to 'ktlint_official'.",
43→        SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
44→        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
45→    )
46→
47→public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<CodeStyleValue> =
48→    EditorConfigProperty(
49→        type = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE,
50→        /*
51→         * Once the [CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official] is matured, it will become the default code style of ktlint. Until
52→         * then the [CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea] is used to remain backwards compatible.
53→         */
54→        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
55→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.android_studio,
56→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
57→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
58→    )
